### Post by bikerman2299 on 2009-03-26
I am trying to get my laptop to recognize my external. Its a WD 2TB preformated HFS+. I can plug it in and I can see it under fdisk -l but I cannot mount it, cannot force mount it, or anything. I even see it under gedit /etc/fstab see's it. check it out....
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
# -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config --
#
# <file system> <mount point> <type> <options> <dump> <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda1 :
UUID=1cc335cc-8cfe-4ca7-b8e0-c39d79dec4e7 / ext3 relatime,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda5 :**<---MY EXTERNAL** 
UUID=a8b6c509-438d-4a61-a8c3-79b07d2558f7 /dev/sda5 hfsplus
/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto,exec,utf8 0 0

this is fdisk -l

255 heads, 63 sectors/track, 12161 cylinders
Units = cylinders of 16065 * 512 = 8225280 bytes

/dev/sda2 11785 12161 3028252+ 5 Extended
/dev/sda5<------ 11785 12161 3028221 82 Linux swap / Solaris

this is what i get if i normally try to mount it.

[ 5162.161949] b43-phy0 ERROR: You must go to [http://linuxwireless.org/en/users/Dr...devicefirmware](http://linuxwireless.org/en/users/Dr...devicefirmware) and download the correct firmware (version 4).

So yeah, if anyone could give me a hand with this. I already have the NTFS Config Tool installed and all it does is pop up a window that say enable NTFS support for external HDD. No configure or anything. I am very new to Ubuntu and could use some help. I have read a tons of posts on here and everything I have tried has failed. Any help would be greatly appreciated. Thanks!

### Post by ddrichardson on 2009-03-27
It can't be /dev/sda5, that's a logical partition on the first interface. What do you get from```
ls /dev/s*
```

Also, I take it your wifi isn't working?

---
